destination.vibe: 90 miles north of Penn Station by Amtrak, Rhinebeck sits at the center of the best road cycling in the Northeast — 300-year-old country lanes through orchards and river estates, the Hudson at the bottom of every hill, the Catskills on the horizon, and the Shawangunk Ridge one bridge-crossing away.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (Tue/Wed cheapest; 4+ daily nonstops DTW→JFK)
flights.notes: DTW→JFK nonstop. Best bike policy of any major carrier: bikes treated as oversized checked bag at the standard rate (~$35/way), not the $150 penalty others charge. Confirm 115 linear inch / 50 lb limit before booking. Average $179–$217 RT in July — book 40 days out for best pricing.

flights.outbound_dates: Early August (Tue Aug 4 cheapest; ~daily nonstops DTW→EWR)
flights.notes: DTW→EWR (Newark). Cheapest ticket but United's bike fee is ~$150 each way — wipes out the savings vs. Delta unless you're shipping your bike separately. EWR is easy for Penn Station: Newark AirTrain + NJ Transit (~40min, ~$18).

flights.outbound_dates: Late July (Jul 29 from $129 one-way; flexible Aug return from ~$149 OW)
flights.notes: DTW→JFK nonstop. $100 bike handling fee + standard bag fee, and an 80-linear-inch / 50 lb hard case limit that's tight for most bike bags. Best used as a backup if Delta is sold out.

transportation.name: AirTrain + Subway — JFK to Penn Station
transportation.description: JFK → AirTrain to Jamaica → A or E train to Midtown Penn Station · bikes allowed on NYC subway
transportation.notes: Bikes are allowed on the NYC subway — carry/fold vertically. Alternative: LIRR from Jamaica to Penn Station (~$11.50, ~30min total) if you want to move faster.

transportation.name: Amtrak Empire Service — Penn Station NYC → Rhinecliff
transportation.description: Penn Station NYC to Rhinecliff station · the scenic route up the east bank of the Hudson River
transportation.notes: The Empire Service runs 3–4x daily to Rhinecliff. Reserve bike space at booking — July fills fast. The last hour follows the Hudson River with Catskills views across the water.

transportation.name: Amtrak — Rhinecliff → Poughkeepsie (Shawangunk day)
transportation.description: 5 stops south to Poughkeepsie · used for the Shawangunk Ridge day ride
transportation.notes: Take this instead of riding 30+ miles south just to reach the Walkway Over the Hudson. Poughkeepsie station is 10 minutes from the Walkway entrance by bike.

lodging.name: Beekman Arms & Delamater Inn
lodging.highlights: America's oldest continuously operating inn (est. 1766) on Market Street — roll out the front door onto back roads, free breakfast, free parking, walking distance to every restaurant and farm stand in town.

lodging.name: The Wick
lodging.highlights: Converted 1860s candle factory on Hudson's gallery-lined Warren Street, 2 miles from the Empire State Trail — complimentary breakfast, Lil' Deb's Oasis and Cafe Mutton are both around the corner.

lodging.name: Mirbeau Inn & Spa
lodging.highlights: The only lodging in the valley explicitly built for the cycling-then-recovery pattern: complimentary loaner bikes, a full spa, pools, on-site dining — plus you're one mile from every back road in Dutchess County.

itinerary.overview: The Hudson Valley has been the best road cycling in the Northeast for decades, and cyclists have been the only ones who know it. The roads around Rhinebeck, Red Hook, and Tivoli are genuinely quiet — traffic stayed on Route 9 while the real roads looped through farm country behind it. You fly into JFK, take the Amtrak Empire Service 90 miles north to Rhinecliff (a Victorian depot right on the riverbank), roll off the train, and you're immediately in back-road territory. Across the Hudson, the Shawangunk Ridge offers some of the best sustained climbing in the Northeast: 15% pitches through white conglomerate escarpments, accessed via the Walkway Over the Hudson in Poughkeepsie.

days.title: Arrive — DTW → JFK → Rhinecliff
days.morning: Fly DTW→JFK (Delta, 1h52m nonstop). AirTrain to Jamaica station (~8min, $8.50), then A or E subway to Penn Station (~35min, $2.90). Or LIRR from Jamaica to Penn Station (~$11.50, faster). Amtrak Empire Service to Rhinecliff — book the bike space at amtrak.com ($10 fee, July fills fast).
days.afternoon: Rhinecliff station is a 1873 Victorian depot right on the river bank. The mile from Rhinecliff up to Rhinebeck village is the one climb of the day. Check in, assemble bike if needed. Bread Alone Bakery (45 E Market St, closes 5pm) for a pastry and coffee.
days.evening: Dinner at Terrapin (farm-to-table since 1998) or casual at Aba's Falafel for something quick.

days.title: Rhinebeck → Bard → Tivoli → Red Hook — 34mi / 55km, ~1,100ft / 335m
days.morning: Head north from Rhinebeck on Route 9G, then onto back roads through Bard College campus. Continue north to Tivoli. Tivoli General (52 Broadway): get a bialy before they sell out.
days.afternoon: Lunch at GioBatta Alimentari (48 Broadway, Tivoli) — Ruth Reichl is a known regular. Head south toward Red Hook, stopping at Montgomery Place Orchards (Route 9G — open Wed–Sat 9–6, Sun 9–5 from mid-June): 60+ apple varieties, apple cider donuts. Return to Rhinebeck.
days.evening: Gigi (Mediterranean with Hudson Valley ingredients) or Rhinebeck Farmers' Market if it's Sunday (10am–2pm).

days.title: River Road Long Loop — 52mi / 84km, ~1,400ft / 427m
days.morning: Early start — 6:30 or 7am. Head southwest toward the river, connecting to River Road south. River Road is the open secret of Hudson Valley cycling: flat, paved, river views, almost no cars. Stop at Migliorelli Farm Stand (668 River Rd, open daily 9–6) for mid-ride fuel.
days.afternoon: Loop back north through inland farm roads and rolling terrain before returning to Rhinebeck.
days.evening: Terrapin in Rhinebeck, or Amtrak one stop south to Cafe Mutton (757 Columbia St, Hudson — crépinettes, beef tartare, Wed–Fri evenings).

days.title: Hudson Day — Warren Street, Olana, Lil' Deb's
days.morning: Ride 25 miles south on Route 9G to Hudson, or take Amtrak one stop south (~12 minutes, ~$12 each way). Circles Bagels in Hudson for breakfast.
days.afternoon: Olana State Historic Site (5720 Route 9G) — Frederic Church's 250-acre Persian-inspired estate. Grounds free to walk; house tour ticketed. Then Warren Street: 50+ antique dealers, Carrie Haddad Gallery (best photography gallery in the valley), Ornamentum.
days.evening: Lil' Deb's Oasis (747 Columbia St, Thu–Sun only, no reservations). Arrive 15 minutes before opening. Tropical comfort food with Hudson Valley ingredients: whole fried fish, plantains, octopus tostadas. James Beard-nominated.

days.title: Shawangunk Ridge — 46mi / 74km, 2,800ft / 853m (from New Paltz)
days.morning: Take Amtrak south to Poughkeepsie (~35min from Rhinecliff, ~$12), then ride 10 minutes to the Walkway Over the Hudson — a 1.28-mile pedestrian/cycling bridge 212 feet above the river, free, open 7am–sunset. Cross to Highland (west bank) and ride 12 miles south to New Paltz.
days.afternoon: Two options: (A) Shawangunk Ridge Road Ride — 46 miles paved, 2,848ft elevation, 15% pitches; (B) Mohonk/Minnewaska State Park carriage roads — 35+ miles no-car gravel, $10 day-use fee. Descend to New Paltz, Amtrak back north from Poughkeepsie.
days.evening: Recovery dinner. Something simple in Rhinebeck.

days.title: Farmers' Market Morning → NYC → DTW
days.morning: If it's Sunday: Rhinebeck Farmers' Market (10am–2pm, 23 E Market St) — the best in the Hudson Valley. Pick up provisions for the train. Amtrak Empire Service south to Penn Station.
days.afternoon: AirTrain and Delta back to DTW. Home by dinner.

tips: River Road goes by multiple names as it runs parallel to the Hudson — download your route from Mid Hudson Bicycle Club (midhudsonbicycleclub.com) or Komoot before leaving; cell signal on back roads can be spotty.
July heat in the Hudson Valley is genuine — mid-80s to low 90s with humidity. Aim to finish riding by noon. Start at 6:30am.
Lil' Deb's Oasis (Hudson) is Thursday–Sunday only and takes no reservations. Go at opening or expect a significant wait. Plan your Hudson day for a Thu–Sun.
GioBatta Alimentari in Tivoli (48 Broadway) doesn't have much of an online presence. Go for lunch — it's the best food in the valley for its size and price point.
The Walkway Over the Hudson is the key to accessing the Shawangunks without a car. Free, open 7am–sunset, bikes allowed.
Mid Hudson Bicycle Club (midhudsonbicycleclub.com) has 700+ published routes on Ride with GPS covering Dutchess and Ulster Counties at every skill level.
Rhinebeck Farmers' Market (Sundays, 10am–2pm) is worth building your itinerary around — it's the social center of the valley on summer weekends.
Montgomery Place Orchards (Route 9G, Red Hook) is explicitly bike-accessible. The apple cider donuts are not optional.

sources.title: Guide to Cycling the Hudson Valley — Epic Road Rides
Mid Hudson Bicycle Club — Ride with GPS route library
Best Restaurants in Rhinebeck — The Infatuation

destination.vibe: 80 miles north of Seattle at the foot of the North Cascades, where Chuckanut Drive clings to the cliffs above Samish Bay, a $2 county ferry puts you on a car-optional island 10 miles from downtown, and SR-542 climbs 4,100 feet through old-growth forest to the Coleman Glacier at Artist Point.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August (Tue/Wed departures cheapest; 5 nonstop flights/week from DTW)
flights.notes: DTW→SEA nonstop. July peak expect $380–470 RT. Bike fee: $30–75 each way — verify at alaskaair.com. Getting SEA→Bellingham: (A) Link Light Rail to King Street Station then Amtrak Cascades to Fairhaven Station (~2h, $27–45, bikes with $10 reservation); (B) Car rental at SEA ($55–80/day), drive I-5 north 80 miles, 90 min. A car required for the Mt. Baker day.

transportation.name: Amtrak Cascades — King Street Station (Seattle) → Fairhaven Station (Bellingham)
transportation.description: Scenic rail along Puget Sound · 4 trains daily · bikes accepted
transportation.notes: Reserve bike space early — July Cascades fill fast. Fairhaven Station is 3 flat miles from downtown Bellingham.

transportation.name: Car Rental at SEA — I-5 north to Bellingham (80 mi, ~90 min)
transportation.description: Seattle-Tacoma Airport → Bellingham · required for the Mt. Baker day
transportation.notes: Reserve early — July inventory is tight.

lodging.name: Hotel Leo
lodging.highlights: 1920s boutique hotel with a confirmed locked bike room, dedicated bike wash station, a prohibition-era cocktail bar, and a private 30-seat screening room.

lodging.name: Heliotrope Hotel
lodging.highlights: Renovated 1950s boutique property at 2419 Elm St with a dedicated bike washing station, seven distinct room layouts, a fire pit yard — 10 minutes from downtown by bike.

lodging.name: Chrysalis Inn & Spa
lodging.highlights: Waterfront hotel on Bellingham Bay with gas fireplaces in every room, on-site spa, hot tub, and Keenan's at the Pier restaurant with direct bay views.

lodging.name: Tiny House — Mt. Baker View
lodging.highlights: Entire guesthouse on a 4-acre wooded property with Mt. Baker views — loft bed, fire pit, projector and sound bar.

lodging.name: Walnut Hut — Farm Stay
lodging.neighborhood: Rural Bellingham (6 miles from downtown, near Lynden)
lodging.highlights: Rustic cabin on a 9-acre permaculture biodynamic farm — outdoor kitchen (April–October), seasonal farmstand, farm tours by appointment.

itinerary.overview: Bellingham is the kind of Pacific Northwest city cyclists move to on purpose. It sits 80 miles north of Seattle on Bellingham Bay, with the North Cascades rising immediately east and Chuckanut Drive — a 23-mile cliff-hugging two-lane above Samish Bay — beginning right at the city's southern edge. The 10-day structure works in three arcs: Bellingham as base (Chuckanut Drive, Lummi Island, a rest day) for four days; the San Juan Islands for three days of island-hopping via Washington State Ferry; and Mt. Baker for the penultimate day — 24 miles and 4,100 feet of climbing up SR-542 to Artist Point at 5,140 feet.

days.title: Arrive — SEA → Bellingham, Fairhaven
days.morning: Fly DTW→SEA on Alaska Airlines (5h48m nonstop). Amtrak Cascades north to Fairhaven Station (2h, bike reservation required) OR rent car at SEA and drive I-5 north (90 min).
days.afternoon: Check in, assemble the bike. Walk Fairhaven Village. Village Books on 11th St is one of the great independent bookstores in the Pacific Northwest.
days.evening: Dinner at Dirty Dan Harris or downtown. First beer at Aslan Brewing (1330 N Forest St) or Wander Brewing (1807 Dean Ave).

days.title: Chuckanut Drive + Skagit Flats — 50mi / 80km, 2,418ft / 737m
days.morning: Roll out 7:30am south through Fairhaven onto Chuckanut Drive (SR-11) — 23 miles of cliff-hugging road above Chuckanut Bay and Samish Bay. Stop at Larrabee State Park (mile 7) for the tidepools.
days.afternoon: Descend into Bow and east into the Skagit Valley farm loop. Lunch at Breadfarm in Bow-Edison: the best bread bakery in northwest Washington.
days.evening: Recovery pint at Wander Brewing or Kulshan Brewing. Dinner at La Fiamma Wood Fire Pizza (200 E Chestnut St).

days.title: Lummi Island via Whatcom Chief Ferry — 47mi / 76km
days.morning: Ride 10 miles south on Haxton Way to Gooseberry Point. Whatcom Chief ferry (Whatcom County vessel, NOT WSF) — ~$2–4 round trip, runs every 30 min, 6-minute crossing.
days.afternoon: Circumnavigate Lummi Island: 12-mile perimeter road with rolling terrain. Return ferry and ride 10 miles back to Bellingham.
days.evening: Wild Buffalo House of Music or the Shakedown.

days.title: Rest Day — Whatcom Falls + Bellingham
days.morning: Whatcom Falls Park: falls pour over a basalt shelf into a canyon pool. Easy trails through the ravine.
days.afternoon: Whatcom Museum Lightcatcher Building (250 Flora St) — rotating contemporary exhibitions. Then rent a kayak on Bellingham Bay at Squalicum Harbor.
days.evening: The Shakedown (1212 N State St, good cocktails, locally sourced menu).

days.title: San Juan Islands, Day 1: Lopez Island — 37mi / 60km
days.morning: Drive to Anacortes ferry terminal (30 miles south, 40 min). WSF to Lopez Island (~1h30m; walk-on $15.85 each way, bike ~$2–4, no reservation needed).
days.afternoon: Loop Lopez: 37–40 miles, gently rolling. Shark Reef Sanctuary (seal rookery views), Spencer Spit State Park (swim). Lunch at Fine Mess Bakery in Lopez Village.
days.evening: Overnight on Lopez. MacKaye Harbor Inn (~$175–200/night) or camping at Odlin County Park ($25–30).

days.title: San Juan Islands, Day 2: Lopez → Orcas, Mt. Constitution — 35mi / 56km, 1,900ft / 580m
days.morning: Inter-island WSF ferry from Lopez to Orcas (~30–45 min). 9 miles through Crow Valley to Eastsound. Stock up on food and water before Moran State Park.
days.afternoon: Mt. Constitution: 5 miles from the park entrance to 2,409-foot summit, switchbacks reaching 15% grades. CCC-built stone observation tower at the top.
days.evening: Overnight on Orcas. Outlook Inn (~$200–250/night). Dinner at Mijita's (310 A St, Eastsound).

days.title: San Juan Islands, Day 3: Orcas → San Juan Island → Bellingham — 40mi / 64km
days.morning: Ferry Orcas to Friday Harbor (~1h30m). Ride southwest along West Side Road to Lime Kiln Point State Park — best land-based orca-watching site in North America (Southern Resident killer whales, June–September).
days.afternoon: English Camp (northwest shore, 1859–1872 Pig War British garrison) and American Camp (south end). Total loop: 35–43 miles. Ferry back to Anacortes, drive north to Bellingham.
days.evening: Back in Bellingham. Rest.

days.title: Recovery — Nooksack Valley Farm Loop
days.morning: Active recovery: flat 25-mile Lynden loop through Whatcom County farm country. Dutch Bakery in Lynden for a mid-ride snack.
days.afternoon: Return to Bellingham. Kayak at Squalicum Harbor or spa at Chrysalis Inn (day passes available).
days.evening: Confirm Mt. Baker road status: call (360) 856-5700 ext. 515 or check nwroadinfo.org.

days.title: Mt. Baker SR-542 Hill Climb — 48mi / 77km roundtrip, 4,100ft / 1,250m
days.morning: Drive 32 miles east to Glacier, WA (50 min). Climb by 8am. SR-542 east 24 miles to Artist Point (5,140ft) — past Nooksack Falls viewpoint (170-foot waterfall, 3-minute stop), through heather meadows to the permanent snowfield.
days.afternoon: Descend 24 miles back to Glacier. Lunch at Graham's Store — the only place to eat in Glacier, solid burgers. Drive back to Bellingham.
days.evening: Final evening in Bellingham. Pack the bike tonight.

days.title: Depart — Bellingham → SEA
days.morning: Tony's Coffee Roastery (2033 Division St — Bellingham's coffee institution since 1971). Amtrak Cascades south to King Street Station, Seattle (2h), then Link Light Rail to SeaTac (40 min). OR drive rental car to SEA (90 min on I-5 south).

tips: Amtrak Cascades bike policy: space is limited and July fills fast — reserve your bike spot ($10 fee) well in advance.
Whatcom Chief ferry (Lummi Island): NOT a Washington State Ferry — Whatcom County vessel. Total cost for a cyclist roughly $2–4 round trip. Runs every 30 minutes 6am–10pm daily.
WSF San Juan Islands: walk-on passengers ($15.85 each way) never need a reservation — cyclists board last and almost always make the next sailing.
SR-542 / Artist Point road status: Artist Point Road typically opens mid-July. Call (360) 856-5700 ext. 515 or check nwroadinfo.org.
July weather in Bellingham: finest month of the year, 65–75°F. Mt. Baker can be 20–30°F colder at 5,000ft — pack a rain shell and arm warmers for Day 9.

sources.title: Bicycles on Washington State Ferries — WSDOT

destination.vibe: A Victorian silver-mining town of 6,500 at 7,036 feet in the Arkansas River Valley, circled by more 14,000-foot peaks per square mile than anywhere in North America — where a 22-mile road climb takes you to the Continental Divide at 11,312 feet, the descent is 4,000 vertical feet of fast pavement back to green chile and hot springs, and the entire town fits on two walkable blocks.

flights.notes: DTW→DEN direct at $124–157 RT. Bag fees trap: checked bike ~$75–100 each way. Total with bike: ~$290–340. Consider BikeFlights.com (~$80–120 each way) and fly carry-on only. Drive to Salida: US-285 south from Denver, ~2.5 hours, 150 miles.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (midweek Tue/Wed cheapest; 24 flights/week DTW→DEN)
flights.notes: DTW→DEN direct. Delta bike fee: $150 each way. BikeFlights is more cost-effective. Pro tip: fly into Colorado Springs (COS) instead — $180–250 RT, 30 minutes closer to Salida.
DTW→ORD or EWR→DEN. United bike fee: $200 each way — most expensive. Strongly consider BikeFlights. Award availability through Chase/Star Alliance often at 5,500–7,500 miles one-way.

lodging.name: Amigo Motor Lodge
lodging.neighborhood: Salida — east end of town near US-50
lodging.highlights: 1960s motor lodge fully renovated — bike parking on-site, balcony or patio with mountain views, hot tub, outdoor fireplace. Michelin Select property. Roll to the Monarch Pass climb in 5 minutes.

lodging.name: Manhattan Hotel Salida
lodging.neighborhood: Downtown Salida — Arkansas River / F Street Arts District
lodging.highlights: Salida's only downtown boutique hotel above the Boathouse Cantina restaurant with direct Arkansas River views. Walking distance to every restaurant and gallery in town.

lodging.name: Frontcountry Salida Micro-Cabins
lodging.highlights: Standalone micro-cabin units with private outdoor spaces and hotel-style housekeeping. July nights drop to 45–55°F — the cabin setup is ideal for cold-morning gear prep.

itinerary.overview: Salida is the cycling anchor of the Arkansas River Valley — a genuine small mountain town at the intersection of three world-class road climbs, two whitewater rivers, and one of the densest concentrations of 14,000-foot peaks in North America. The riding is structured around July's weather pattern: brilliant mornings followed by afternoon thunderstorms arriving 1–3pm. Start climbs early, summit before noon, be descending when the sky turns.

days.title: Monarch Pass — 46mi / 74km out-and-back, 4,258ft / 1,298m gain
days.morning: Wheels rolling by 7am from downtown Salida. West on US-50 through Poncha Springs (5 miles, flat warmup). Monarch Pass climb: 18 miles to the Continental Divide at 11,312 ft, grade averages 4.3% with sections to 7% in the canyon narrows. Summit lodge sells coffee and hot chocolate — genuinely cold up there in July (40–50°F).
days.afternoon: Descent back to Salida: 18 miles of fast pavement. Back by noon. Walk the F Street Arts District. The Salida Hot Springs Aquatic Center (410 W Rainbow Blvd, ~$10) is geothermal-fed and perfect post-ride.
days.evening: Dinner: Amica's Pizzeria (wood-fired) or Fritz (European bistro, excellent wine list for a town of 6,500). Walk to the river trail — evening light on the Collegiate Peaks.

days.title: Cottonwood Pass — 67mi / 108km, 4,100ft / 1,250m gain
days.morning: Drive 28 miles north to Buena Vista (35 min on US-285). Park at the base of County Road 306 (elevation 7,965 ft). Cottonwood Pass: 19.5 miles to 12,126 ft — third-highest paved road in Colorado. Grade averages 4.1% with a 10% wall at roughly mile 14. East side climbs through aspen groves and spruce forest for 12 miles, then breaks into open tundra.
days.afternoon: Descend east back to Buena Vista, or explore the west side down to Taylor Park Reservoir (13.5 miles, 2,700 ft). Lunch at Eddyline Brewery on the river.
days.evening: Return to Salida. Soak at Salida Hot Springs or Mount Princeton Hot Springs (25 miles north, ~$30). Dinner: Soulcraft Brewing (downtown, excellent smoked brisket).

days.title: Poncha Pass + San Luis Valley traverse — 62mi / 100km, 1,800ft / 550m gain
days.morning: South on US-285 to Poncha Springs (5 miles) and climb to Poncha Pass (9,010 ft). At the summit: the San Luis Valley — 8,000 square miles of flat high-altitude basin at 7,500 ft, ringed by mountains, the Rio Grande through the center. Ride south 20–25 miles into the vast flat valley.
days.afternoon: Return north over Poncha Pass back to Salida. Back by noon or early afternoon. Afternoon: Salida Museum (free), Arkansas Headwaters Recreation Area visitor center, or riverside café watching rafters.
days.evening: Last evening: Tenderfoot Mountain ('S' Mountain) — 20-minute hike to the best view of the Collegiate Peaks and Arkansas River valley at golden hour. Dinner at Moonlight Pizza & Brewpub.

tips: Colorado afternoon thunderstorms are a hard stop: start Monarch and Cottonwood by 7am, summit by 10–10:30am, be well into descent before noon.
Bike transport: BikeFlights.com (~$80–120 each way) is strongly recommended over airline fees ($150–200 each way). Ship to the hotel 4–5 days before arrival.
Car rental: you need a car in Salida. Reserve early — July is peak Colorado summer and inventory is tight.
Altitude: Salida is at 7,036 feet, Monarch climbs to 11,312 feet. Plan one easy day before the big climbs. Drink a liter of water before your first ride morning.
Salida Hot Springs Aquatic Center (410 W Rainbow Blvd, ~$10/day) is geothermal-fed — open daily, perfect post-ride recovery.
The 'S' Mountain hike: 20-minute trail to the summit of Tenderfoot Mountain with the best view of the Collegiate Peaks at sunset.

destination.vibe: A 62,000-person mountain town where Chinese Nationalist soldiers stranded after Mao's 1949 victory planted oolong tea on 1,200-meter ridges and built a Yunnan village that has nothing to do with Thailand — and a 108km road loop climbing 1,700 meters through it waits 40km from your guesthouse, morning clear before the rains arrive.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (Tue/Wed departures cheapest; book 6–8 weeks out)
flights.notes: DTW → ICN (Seoul Incheon) → BKK (Bangkok Suvarnabhumi). Add domestic: Thai AirAsia or Nok Air BKK (Don Mueang DMK) → CEI, ~$30–45 each way.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (midweek best; book 6–8 weeks out)
flights.notes: DTW → TPE (Taipei Taoyuan) → BKK. Add domestic BKK → CEI separately (~$30–45).

flights.outbound_dates: August (slightly cheaper than July; book 6–8 weeks out)
flights.notes: DTW → HKG (Hong Kong) → BKK. Add domestic BKK → CEI separately.

lodging.name: Bed & Bike Hostel (Poshtel)
lodging.neighborhood: Chiang Rai city center — near Clock Tower
lodging.highlights: The only cycling-dedicated accommodation in Chiang Rai — bike hire, secure bike storage, coffee shop on-site. Private rooms from ~$25–30/night.

lodging.name: Wiang Inn Hotel
lodging.neighborhood: Chiang Rai city center — 5 min walk to night market
lodging.highlights: Classic mid-range choice — outdoor pool, free breakfast, free parking. On Phahonyothin Road, the main artery heading north toward the Mae Salong climb.

lodging.name: The Legend Chiang Rai Boutique River Resort & Spa
lodging.neighborhood: Kok River bank — 10 min from city center
lodging.highlights: Boutique bungalow resort on the Kok River — spa, pool, riverside dining. Bike rentals on-site.

itinerary.overview: Chiang Rai sits at 580 meters in Thailand's far north, surrounded by mountain ranges pushing to 1,600 meters along the borders with Myanmar and Laos. A working provincial town of 62,000, not a resort city. July is rainy season: reliable morning windows (7am–noon) followed by afternoon thunderstorms.

days.title: Doi Mae Salong Loop — 108km / 67mi, 1,729m climbing
days.morning: Wheels by 7am north on Route 1 toward Mae Chan (34km, flat warmup). Turn west onto Route 1130 and climb 25km to Santikhiri (Mae Salong) at 1,200m — a Yunnan Chinese enclave founded by KMT soldiers who fled Mao's China in 1949. Tea and khao soi before the descent.
days.afternoon: Descend south side via Route 1234 toward Ban Hin Taek, then east to close the loop. Back in town by 1pm. Visit Wat Rong Khun (the White Temple) 13km south.
days.evening: Night Bazaar (6–11pm). Khao soi, larb moo, sai oua sausage. Dinner ~$5–7.

days.title: Doi Tung Climb + Golden Triangle — 95km / 59mi, 1,300m climbing
days.morning: Depart 6:30am north on Route 110. At the Doi Tung junction (35km north), west onto the mountain road. Climb 18km to 1,389m through the Mae Fah Luang Royal Project — former opium zone transformed into macadamia and tea farms.
days.afternoon: Descend through Mae Sai border town. Continue east on Route 1016 (Mekong River Road) to the Golden Triangle. The Hall of Opium museum (800 THB, 2 hours) at Sop Ruak is one of Southeast Asia's best.
days.evening: Riverside dinner in Sop Ruak watching longtail boats on the Mekong. Return to Chiang Rai by songthaew or van.

days.title: Chiang Saen River Loop via Mekong Road — 80km / 50mi, 400m climbing
days.morning: North on Route 1016. Chiang Saen (55km): Lanna Kingdom's former capital with intact walls and moat, Wat Chedi Luang ruins (1328, 58m chedi), small National Museum.
days.afternoon: Continue to Sop Ruak for the Golden Triangle viewpoint, then south back toward Chiang Rai.
days.evening: Saturday Night Walking Street on Thanalai Road if timing works (6–11pm, local food and hill-tribe vendors).

tips: July rain timing: mornings clear until about 11am–noon, afternoon thunderstorms most days 2–6pm. Start all rides by 7am.
Mae Salong grades: up to 22–24% on steepest ramps. Route 1130 surface is well-maintained. Clockwise (north side up, south via Ban Hin Taek) offers more consistent grades.
Bike logistics on domestic leg: Thai AirAsia and Nok Air accept bikes on BKK→CEI. Book sports baggage allowance when purchasing ticket.
Hall of Opium (Sop Ruak): funded by the Thai royal family, staffed by serious historians. Allow 2 full hours. ~800 THB (~$22).
Hydration: July mountain humidity means electrolytes matter. Thai 7-Eleven stocks Pocari Sweat and coconut water everywhere including mountain villages.

sources.title: Doi Mae Salong Chiang Rai road ride — Bicycle Thailand
The Golden Frontier Chiang Rai Tour — Cycling Chiang Mai

destination.vibe: Home turf of Tadej Pogačar — where 50 cobbled hairpins climb to 1,611 meters on a road built by Russian WWI prisoners, the Soča runs glacier-turquoise through raw limestone gorges, and summer temperatures in the low 20s mean you can push 80-mile days in the Julian Alps without a 5am alarm.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (Tue/Wed departures cheapest; book 4–6 weeks out)
flights.notes: Connects through Frankfurt (FRA→LJU). Bike fee €100 each way.
Connects through Vienna (VIE→LJU, ~1h). Bike fee €75–100 each way.
Connects through Zurich (ZRH→LJU, ~1h30m). Bike fee €80–100 each way.

lodging.name: Reset Apartments — Bike Resort Kranjska Gora
lodging.highlights: Officially designated 'Bike Resort' — secure indoor bike storage, wash station, drying room. Walking distance to the Vršič Pass road start.

lodging.name: Hotel Kompas Kranjska Gora
lodging.highlights: Slovenia's premier cyclist-specialist hotel — certified cycling accommodation with secure storage, assembly area, pool and wellness.

lodging.name: Hotel Cubo Ljubljana
lodging.neighborhood: Ljubljana Old Town — 400m from Triple Bridge
lodging.highlights: Cycle-friendly certified, confirmed secure bike storage. Perfect first/last night in the city before heading to the Julian Alps.

itinerary.overview: Ljubljana sits at the foot of the Julian Alps, 50km from roads that shaped Tadej Pogačar. Within a day's ride you're at 1,611 meters on the Vršič Pass — 50 hairpin turns, cobbled bends 5–9, and the Soča Valley glacier-turquoise below. July temperatures average 20–25°C at valley level.

days.title: Ljubljana → Bled via Sava Valley — 75 miles / 120km, 1,200m climbing
days.morning: Roll northwest from the Dragon Bridge along the Sava River. Stop in Kranj (32km) on a limestone peninsula above the gorge. Continue through Lesce to the Radovna Valley approach to Bled.
days.afternoon: Lake Bled circuit (6km, flat). Climb to Ojstrica viewpoint (20% pitch, best overhead Bled view). Optional Pokljuka Plateau extension: 18km, 800m gain, biathlon venue.
days.evening: Dinner at Gostilna Pri Planincu (Grajska cesta 8). Up to Bled Castle for the night view of the Alps.

days.title: Vršič Pass Queen Stage — 88km / 55 miles, 1,900m climbing
days.morning: Start Kranjska Gora 7am. Vršič climb (12km to 1,611m): bends 5–9 are the famous cobbled section at 15–17% on original 1916 WWI prisoner-built road surface. Pause at bend #8 for the Russian Orthodox Chapel. Summit: 50 hairpins — the most of any pass in Europe.
days.afternoon: Descend 26 hairpins into Trenta Valley. Detour 3km to the Soča source — cold blue-green pool erupting from a limestone grotto. Follow the valley 26km to Bovec along the glacier-blue Soča.
days.evening: Lunch in Bovec (Bovška trout, polenta). Wade into the Soča: 8–12°C river is the best ice-bath in the Alps.

days.title: Mangart Saddle — Slovenia's Highest Drivable Road — 90km / 56 miles, 2,200m climbing
days.morning: Depart Bovec 7am. Turn east at Koritnica Valley junction toward the Mangart toll gate (~€10; cyclists typically waved through). Climb 10km to 2,072m through five dark tunnels (front light required), grades to 25%, views into Austria and Italy from the saddle.
days.afternoon: Descend to Bovec. South to Kobarid (12km) — site of Hemingway's A Farewell to Arms. Kobarid Museum (European Museum of the Year 1993) is one of the best WWI museums in Europe.
days.evening: Return to Kranjska Gora or Ljubljana for flight connection. Kranjska Gora to LJU: 85km (1h15m).

tips: Vršič cobbled hairpins (bends 5–9): commit to your gear before the cobbles start — a shift at 15% on rough stone can snap a chain. Wide tires (28–32c) handle this more confidently.
Mangart tunnel lighting is mandatory: five dark tunnels, longest ~90 seconds at climbing pace.
Alpine afternoon weather: July thunderstorms develop fast over Triglav by 1–2pm. Start before 7:30am, be off exposed ridges by noon.
Soča River recovery: 8–12°C even in July. A 10-minute wade after Day 2 is the best ice-bath you'll find at altitude.

sources.title: Cycling Slovenia's Julian Alps — Epic Road Rides
Vršič Pass climb stats — Pjamm Cycling

destination.vibe: The flattest big city in Europe, wrapped in Moorish palaces and orange trees, with 180km of dedicated bike lanes connecting neighborhoods that haven't changed in 500 years — you just have to be on the road before the sun gets serious.

flights.notes: Connects through Istanbul (IST). Bike fees typically €50–80 each way.
Connects through Madrid (MAD→SVQ, 1h15m shuttle). Bike fees €50–100 each way.
Connects through Amsterdam (AMS→SVQ ~3h). Bike fee €50–75 each way.

lodging.name: La Banda Rooftop Hostel
lodging.highlights: Steps from Torre del Oro and Guadalquivir riverfront. Rooftop terrace with Cathedral views.

lodging.name: Hotel Palacio Alcázar
lodging.highlights: 19th-century palace 200m from the Real Alcázar walls. Interior parking for bikes.

lodging.name: Hotel Corral del Rey
lodging.highlights: 17th-century merchant's townhouse, four courtyards, 13 rooms. Secure bike storage. The most atmospheric address in Seville.

itinerary.overview: Seville is one of the great surprises of European cycling — 180km of separated bike lanes through a city of Moorish palaces, orange trees, and flamenco bars. Completely flat. The catch: July temperatures hit 40–43°C by noon. All serious cycling must happen before 9am or after 6:30pm.

destination.vibe: The birthplace of democracy, tragedy, and the gyro — where a 2,500-year-old citadel glows gold at sunset over a city of five million, a souvlaki costs €4, and nothing serious happens before 9pm.

lodging.name: City Circus Athens
lodging.highlights: 9.6/10 on Hostelworld. Neoclassical building, steps from Monastiraki Metro. 2026 Hoscar Award winner.

lodging.name: Sweet Home Hotel
lodging.highlights: 9.3/10 on Booking (1,600 reviews). Elegant rooms 400m from Syntagma Square, some with Acropolis views, homemade breakfast.

lodging.name: AthensWas Design Hotel
lodging.highlights: 21 rooms on the pedestrian promenade directly below the Acropolis with unobstructed Parthenon views.

itinerary.overview: Athens is not a museum with a city around it — it's a city of five million that happens to have an intact 5th-century BC temple on its central hill.

destination.vibe: The city that taught Europe to eat — 900 years of university life, the world's longest portico, tagliatelle al ragù that has nothing to do with 'spaghetti bolognese,' and terracotta buildings so red they named a political color after them.

The city that went from narco capital to Most Innovative City on Earth in one generation — and is now doing it again with food, coffee, street art, and a metro system that's become a point of civic pride.
No nonstops, no problem — Bangkok rewards the extra hours in transit with 12 million people, temples against skyscrapers, Michelin street food for $1, and the baht weaker than it's been in years.
Twenty-two million people, the world's best street tacos, Diego Rivera murals on government buildings, Frida's blue house, Aztec ruins under a baroque cathedral, mezcal bars in converted mansions.

Seven hills, a 1755 earthquake that flattened everything and got rebuilt fast, 600 years of seafaring bravado, and the most melancholic pop music genre ever invented — all held together by custard tarts and cheap wine.
A city that does everything well and brags about none of it — temples next to bubble tea next to Michelin stalls next to the world's best convenience stores.
Ten million people, 600 years of history, and the world's most obsessive food culture — all running at 5G.
